card.coreExplanation: because 用来直接说明原因，后面必须接完整从句：要有主语和谓语，如 because I was tired。它常放在主句后面说明理由，也可放句首先交代原因；放句首时，原因从句后通常要加逗号。
card.usageNote: 语气中性、非常常用，口语和写作都自然，适合回答“为什么”、解释决定、说明情况。它比 since/as 更直接、更明确。若后面只有名词、代词或动名词，不用 because，要改用 because of。句首 because 从句后一般加逗号；句中通常不加。初学者最安全的做法是先写主句，再接 because + 完整从句。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 写结果、决定或情况。常见错误：主句不完整，只剩一个词。例：I stayed home

slotSchema.label: because原因从句
slotSchema.hint: because 后必须是完整从句，要有主语+谓语。常见错误：写成 because the rain。例：because it was raining

slotSchema.label: 句首原因从句
slotSchema.hint: 放句首时，用 Because + 从句, 主句。常见错误：漏逗号。例：Because I was tired, I went to bed early

commonErrors: because of the rain I stayed home → 错在把 because of 结构当 because 从句用；because 后不能直接接名词。改为 I stayed home because of the rain / I stayed home because it was raining.
I stayed home because tired → 错在 because 后没有完整从句，缺主语和谓语。改为 I stayed home because I was tired.
Because I was tired. → 错在只有原因从句，没有主句，成了残句。改为 Because I was tired, I went to bed early.
Because it was late so we left. → 错在 because 和 so 重复表达因果，基础句里通常二选一。改为 Because it was late, we left. / It was late, so we left.
I went to bed early, because I was tired. → 错在普通短句中主句后 because 前通常不加逗号。改为 I went to bed early because I was tired.
Because of I was sick, I missed class. → 错在 because of 后误接从句。改为 Because I was sick, I missed class. / Because of my illness, I missed class.

contrasts.title: because of + 名词/动名词
contrasts.note: 结构区别：because + 完整从句；because of + 名词、代词或动名词。语气都常用，但 because 更适合把原因说完整。对比：I left because I was sick. / I left because of my illness.

contrasts.title: since + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上都接从句；语气上 since 较轻，常表示“既然/由于大家已知的理由”，because 更直接回答真正原因。对比：Since it's raining, let's stay in. / I stayed in because it was raining.

contrasts.title: as + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上也接从句；语气上 as 更书面、更弱，常把原因当背景放前面，because 更明确。对比：As it was getting late, we left. / We left because it was getting late.

contrasts.title: due to + 名词
contrasts.note: 结构区别：due to 后接名词短语，不接完整从句；语体上更正式，常见于通知、报道、正式写作。对比：The game was canceled due to rain. / The game was canceled because it was raining.

contrasts.title: because vs so
contrasts.note: 结构区别：because 引出原因从句，so 引出结果分句。语义方向不同，初学者不要同句硬连两个词。对比：I stayed home because I was sick. / I was sick, so I stayed home.

examples.sentence: I stayed home because I was sick.
examples.translation: 我待在家里，因为我生病了。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型：主句 + because + 完整从句。

examples.sentence: Because it was raining, we took a taxi.
examples.translation: 因为下雨了，我们坐了出租车。
examples.note: because 从句放句首时，后面通常加逗号。

examples.sentence: She didn't go to school because she felt ill.
examples.translation: 她没去上学，因为她觉得不舒服。
examples.note: 日常说明原因，很自然。

examples.sentence: I canceled the meeting because I was busy, not because I was angry.
examples.translation: 我取消会议是因为我很忙，不是因为我生气了。
examples.note: 适合做对比，澄清真正原因。

examples.sentence: Because the data were incomplete, the report was delayed.
examples.translation: 由于数据不完整，报告被推迟了。
examples.note: 较书面，但仍然自然。

examples.sentence: He smiled because he saw his daughter.
examples.translation: 他笑了，因为他看到了女儿。
examples.note: 简单日常、容易模仿。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
templates.template: [主句] because [主语 + 谓语].
slotHints: 先写结果或动作
because 后一定写完整从句，不要只写名词
templates.exampleSentence: I left early because I was tired.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我提前离开了，因为我累了。

templates.label: 句首原因模板
templates.template: Because [主语 + 谓语], [主句].
slotHints: 先交代原因
because 从句后记得加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: Because it was late, we went home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为很晚了，我们回家了。

templates.label: 日常解释模板
templates.template: I [动作] because [主语 + 谓语].
slotHints: 主语常用 I，最容易造句
原因从句可用 was/were, did, could 等完整谓语
templates.exampleSentence: I stayed inside because it was cold.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我待在室内，因为天很冷。

templates.label: 写作型模板
templates.template: Because [原因从句], [结果主句].
slotHints: 适合把原因放前面，显得更有组织
用于说明决定、变化、延迟等
templates.exampleSentence: Because the road was closed, the bus arrived late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为道路封闭，公交车晚到了。

card.coreExplanation: since + 从句表示“既然/由于”，把原因当作说话双方已知、容易接受的背景信息。后面必须接完整从句，不能直接接名词。它常放句首，引出背景后再说主句；也可放句中，但句首更清楚、更自然。
card.usageNote: 语气多为中性到稍正式，常见于解释决定、推理、通知、书面说明，如：既然时间不够，我们先做重点。不适合强调“新发现、最核心、最强烈”的原因，那时常用 because。since 从句在句首后通常加逗号；放句中时注意主句完整。初学者最稳妥的做法是先用“Since + 完整句子, 主句”。

slotSchema.label: 已知背景原因从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句子，要有主语和谓语，如 since it is raining。常错：只放名词，如 since the rain。小例：Since we are late, ...

slotSchema.label: 结果/决定主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放结论、安排、判断等主句。常错：主句不完整或与前面无逻辑关系。小例：..., we should leave now.

commonErrors: ❌ since the rain, we stayed home. 原因：since 表示原因时后面要接完整从句，不直接接名词。✅ since it was raining, we stayed home. 或 because of the rain, we stayed home.
❌ Because since it was late, we left. 原因：because 和 since 都引原因，不能这样重复堆叠。✅ Since it was late, we left. / Because it was late, we left.
❌ We left since, it was late. 原因：since 不能单独后置再接逗号；它要直接连从句。✅ We left since it was late. / Since it was late, we left.
❌ Since was late, we took a taxi. 原因：从句缺主语，不是完整句。✅ Since we were late, we took a taxi.
❌ Since I suddenly found the answer, I was really surprised. 原因：这里强调新发生、最直接的原因，since 不够自然。✅ Because I suddenly found the answer, I was really surprised.
❌ Since of the traffic, he missed the train. 原因：since 后不能接 of + 名词结构。✅ Because of the traffic, he missed the train. / Since there was heavy traffic, he missed the train.

contrasts.title: vs because + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接完整从句，但 because 更适合强调“真正原因/新信息”，since 更像“既然大家都知道”。语气上 because 更直接，since 更像背景铺垫。对比：Because it was raining, we canceled the game.（强调原因）/ Since it was raining, we stayed inside.（把雨当背景）

contrasts.title: vs as + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上都可放句首接完整从句。语气上 as 更轻、更书面，原因存在感更弱；since 比 as 更清楚一点。场景上写作里两者都可见。对比：As it was getting late, we left. / Since it was getting late, we left. 后者“既然已经晚了”的感觉更明显。

contrasts.title: vs because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构区别最大：since + 从句；because of + 名词/代词/v-ing，不接完整句。场景上如果你只有一个名词原因，就别用 since。对比：Because of the rain, we stayed home. / Since it was raining, we stayed home.

contrasts.title: vs due to + noun
contrasts.note: 结构上 due to 后接名词短语，不接从句；语体上更正式，常见于公告、报告。since 较自然，适合一般解释。对比：The delay was due to fog. / Since there was fog, the flight was delayed.

contrasts.title: vs since 表示“自从”
contrasts.note: 同一个词，结构相似但意思不同。原因 since = 既然/由于；时间 since = 自从。看主句最容易分：时间 since 常与现在完成时连用。对比：Since you are here, let's start.（原因）/ Since you came here, he has been happier.（时间）

examples.sentence: Since it is raining, we should stay inside.
examples.translation: 既然在下雨，我们应该待在室内。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型：句首 since 从句 + 主句。

examples.sentence: We should stay inside since it is raining.
examples.translation: 我们应该待在室内，因为在下雨。
examples.note: 同一意思的句中位置；口语里也能用，但句首更像背景说明。

examples.sentence: Since the data are incomplete, the report cannot be finalized today.
examples.translation: 由于数据不完整，这份报告今天无法定稿。
examples.note: 较书面、正式一点的用法。

examples.sentence: Since everyone agrees, let's move to the next point.
examples.translation: 既然大家都同意，我们就进入下一点吧。
examples.note: 适合表示“已知前提下的决定”；这里比 because 更自然。

examples.sentence: Since you have a car, can you drive us to the station?
examples.translation: 既然你有车，你能开车送我们去车站吗？
examples.note: 日常请求中，把原因当共同已知事实。

examples.sentence: Since I was feeling sick, I went home early.
examples.translation: 由于我感觉不舒服，我提前回家了。
examples.note: 日常解释原因；若想强烈强调原因，也可换 because。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: Since + 完整原因从句, + 主句
slotHints: 先写双方容易接受的背景原因，如 we are late / it is raining
主句写决定、建议、结果，如 we should leave now
templates.exampleSentence: Since we are late, we should take a taxi.
templates.exampleTranslation: 既然我们迟到了，我们应该打车。

templates.label: 句中位置模板
templates.template: 主句 + since + 完整原因从句
slotHints: 先说结果，再补背景原因
不要在 since 前后乱加逗号，除非句子很长需要停顿
templates.exampleSentence: I will stay home since I have a fever.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于我发烧了，我会待在家。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: Since + 主语 + be/动词..., + 主句（较正式表达）
slotHints: since 从句可用较正式词汇，如 data are incomplete
主句常写报告、安排、判断，如 cannot be finalized / will be postponed
templates.exampleSentence: Since the schedule has changed, the meeting will be postponed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于日程已经改变，会议将被推迟。

card.coreExplanation: although 用来表达“虽然……但是……”，先让步，再说主要结果。although 后面必须接完整句子，通常要有主语和谓语，如 although it was late。它常放句首，后面主句前常用逗号；也可放句中连接两个分句。
card.usageNote: 语气偏中性到较正式，口语能用，但在说明、作文、邮件、报告里尤其自然，适合表达“承认一个事实，但结论相反”。不适合直接接名词、动词原形或只接一个短语；那时多用 despite / in spite of。若 although 从句在前，后面一般加逗号；放中间时通常不在 although 前加逗号。初学者先稳用模板：Although + 完整句子, + 主句。

slotSchema.label: although 从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句子，要有主语+谓语。常见错误是只放名词或短语，如 although the rain。应改为 although it was raining。例：although she was tired

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放真正想表达的结果或结论。常见错误是主句不完整。应写成完整句子。例：she finished the work

slotSchema.label: 可选位置形式
slotSchema.hint: 可用句首 Although + 从句, 主句；也可用主句 although + 从句。常见错误是前置从句后忘记逗号。例：Although it was cold, we went out

commonErrors: although + 名词：错在 although 后不能直接接名词短语。应改为 although + 完整句子，或改用 despite + 名词。例：Although the rain, we went out → Although it was raining, we went out / Despite the rain, we went out。
Although..., but...：错在英语里 although 已经表达“虽然”，主句通常不再加 but。应去掉 but。例：Although he was tired, but he kept working → Although he was tired, he kept working。
although + to do：错在 although 后不能直接接不定式。应改为完整从句。例：Although to be tired, she continued → Although she was tired, she continued。
前置 although 从句后不加逗号：书写上常不规范，尤其在正式写作里。应写成 Although + 从句, 主句。例：Although it was late we stayed → Although it was late, we stayed。
把 even if 当 although 用：even if 表“即使，假设”，although 表“虽然，事实”。如果说的是已经存在的事实，应改用 although。例：Even if he was sick, he came → Although he was sick, he came。
主句和从句内容不成让步关系：although 需要“前后有反差”。若只是顺承，不自然。例：Although I was hungry, I ate dinner 不如 Because I was hungry, I ate dinner；若要对比，可说 Although I was hungry, I waited until dinner。

contrasts.title: although vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接完整句子。语气上 even though 更强、更强调“尽管事实如此”，although 更中性、更常见于书面。对比：Although it was expensive, I bought it. / Even though it was expensive, I bought it. 后者强调更强。

contrasts.title: although vs despite
contrasts.note: 结构不同：although + 完整句子；despite + 名词/代词/v-ing。语体上 despite 更紧凑，常见于书面。对比：Although he was ill, he worked. / Despite being ill, he worked.

contrasts.title: although vs in spite of
contrasts.note: 结构不同：although 后接从句；in spite of 后接名词或 v-ing。语气上 in spite of 略更正式、固定。对比：Although the weather was bad, we left. / In spite of the bad weather, we left.

contrasts.title: although vs even if
contrasts.note: 结构上都接完整句子；意义不同。although 说真实让步，even if 说假设让步。对比：Although it rains, we will go.（知道在下雨）/ Even if it rains, we will go.（假设下雨也去）

contrasts.title: although vs but
contrasts.note: 结构上 although 引导从句，but 连接两个并列分句。语气上 although 更像“先退一步再转折”，but 更直接。对比：Although I was busy, I replied. / I was busy, but I replied.

examples.sentence: Although it was late, we kept studying.
examples.translation: 虽然很晚了，我们还是继续学习。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：句首 although 从句 + 逗号 + 主句。

examples.sentence: I went for a walk although it was raining.
examples.translation: 虽然在下雨，我还是去散步了。
examples.note: although 从句放在后面也可以，语气更自然口语一些。

examples.sentence: Although the plan is simple, it is highly effective.
examples.translation: 虽然这个方案很简单，但它非常有效。
examples.note: 常见于说明文、报告、正式写作。

examples.sentence: Although he was tired, he kept working.
examples.translation: 虽然他很累，但他继续工作。
examples.note: 适合和 even though 比较：这里用 although 语气较中性。

examples.sentence: Although I don't like coffee, I had some this morning.
examples.translation: 虽然我不喜欢咖啡，但今天早上我还是喝了一些。
examples.note: 日常生活中表达“承认事实但结果相反”。

examples.sentence: Although she had little experience, the team trusted her.
examples.translation: 虽然她经验不多，但团队信任她。
examples.note: 让步内容在前，真正重点通常在后面的主句。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
templates.template: Although + 从句, + 主句.
slotHints: 从句写完整：主语+谓语，如 it was late
主句写结果或决定，如 we stayed
templates.exampleSentence: Although it was cold, we went out.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然很冷，我们还是出去了。

templates.label: 后置从句模板
templates.template: 主句 + although + 从句.
slotHints: 先说主要信息，如 I went for a walk
although 后仍要完整句子，如 it was raining
templates.exampleSentence: I went for a walk although it was raining.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然在下雨，我还是去散步了。

templates.label: 写作常用模板
templates.template: Although + 从句, + 主句 + in fact / still / even now.
slotHints: 前面放让步事实，如 the task was difficult
后面放更想强调的结论，可加 still 等副词
templates.exampleSentence: Although the task was difficult, we still finished it on time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然任务很难，我们还是按时完成了。

card.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来表达“如果某个真实、可能发生的条件成立，就会出现某结果”。if后面用一般现在时，不用will；主句常用will/can+动词原形。if从句可放句首，也可放主句后；放句首时后面通常加逗号。
card.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合计划安排、提醒、承诺、说明结果、给建议。它谈的是现实可发生的未来，不适合表达不太可能或假想情况；那时更常用if+过去式, would...。注意：if从句里别再放will表示将来；句首if从句后通常加逗号。初学者最稳妥的写法是先写“If + 一般现在时, 主语 + will/can + 动词原形”。

slotSchema.label: if-clause
slotSchema.hint: 这里放一般现在时从句，表示条件。常见错误：用will，如If it will rain。应改为If it rains。例：if you study tonight

slotSchema.label: result clause
slotSchema.hint: 这里放结果，常用will/can+动词原形。常见错误：动词不原形，如will goes。应改为will go。例：you will pass the test

slotSchema.label: will/can
slotSchema.hint: 常用will表示结果、承诺、预测；can表示可能或能力。常见错误：两边都用will。例：If you call now, I can help.

commonErrors: If it will rain, we will stay home.｜错因：if从句里一般不用will表示将来。｜改法：If it rains, we will stay home.
If you study, you will passes.｜错因：will后面必须接动词原形。｜改法：If you study, you will pass.
You will be late if will you leave now.｜错因：if从句不用will，语序也不是疑问句语序。｜改法：You will be late if you leave now.
If he comes tomorrow we can to start.｜错因：can后面不能加to。｜改法：If he comes tomorrow, we can start.
If I knew her number, I will call her.｜错因：前半句是过去式，变成假设条件，不是这张卡的真实条件。｜改法：真实条件：If I know her number, I will call her；假设条件：If I knew her number, I would call her.
If you are tired, rest early.｜错因：不算语法错误，但若你想练本结构，主句缺少will/can。｜改法：If you are tired, you should rest early；或If you are tired, you can rest early.

contrasts.title: unless + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上unless直接表示“除非/如果不”，常替代if...not；语气更简洁。适合规则、提醒，不适合所有带否定的if句机械替换。对比：If you don't hurry, you'll miss the bus.=Unless you hurry, you'll miss the bus.

contrasts.title: if + past, would + base
contrasts.note: 结构区别：真实条件用if+现在时, will/can；不真实或较难实现的假设用if+过去式, would。语气上后者更假想、更委婉。对比：If it rains, we'll stay home. / If it rained, we'd stay home.

contrasts.title: as long as + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接从句，但as long as强调“只要这个条件满足”，语气更像前提；if只是一般“如果”。对比：If you finish early, call me. / As long as you finish early, call me.

contrasts.title: when vs if
contrasts.note: 结构都可接现在时从句谈将来，但when更像“到时候会发生”，if强调“是否发生还不确定”。语气上when更确定。对比：When he arrives, we'll eat. / If he arrives, we'll eat.

examples.sentence: If it rains, we will stay home.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就会待在家。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的真实条件句。

examples.sentence: We will stay home if it rains.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就会待在家。
examples.note: if从句放后面也很自然，通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: If you finish the report today, I can review it tonight.
examples.translation: 如果你今天完成报告，我今晚可以审阅。
examples.note: 主句用can，表示能力或可行性。

examples.sentence: If I know the answer, I will tell you. If I knew the answer, I would tell you.
examples.translation: 如果我知道答案，我会告诉你。／如果我知道答案的话，我就会告诉你。
examples.note: 前句是真实条件；后句是假设条件，对比语感很重要。

examples.sentence: If you are hungry, you can make a sandwich.
examples.translation: 如果你饿了，你可以做个三明治。
examples.note: 日常建议场景很常见。

examples.sentence: If payment arrives by Friday, we will ship your order immediately.
examples.translation: 如果款项在周五前到账，我们将立即发货。
examples.note: 较书面、商务风格的用法。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: If + 一般现在时, + 主语 + will + 动词原形
slotHints: 条件用现在时，不用will
结果用will+原形
templates.exampleSentence: If you study tonight, you will feel more prepared tomorrow.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你今晚学习，明天你会觉得更有准备。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
templates.template: 主语 + will + 动词原形 + if + 一般现在时
slotHints: 先说结果，再补条件
if从句放后面通常不加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: I will call you if I arrive early.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果我到得早，我会给你打电话。

templates.label: Can-result template
templates.template: If + 一般现在时, + 主语 + can + 动词原形
slotHints: 主句用can表示可以、能够
适合建议、可行性、能力
templates.exampleSentence: If you have questions, you can email me.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你有问题，可以给我发邮件。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: If + 名词/主语 + 动词现在时 + 时间/条件, + 主语 + will + 动词原形
slotHints: 适合通知、邮件、规则说明
句首if从句后记得逗号
templates.exampleSentence: If your application is complete, we will contact you within five business days.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你的申请材料完整，我们将在五个工作日内联系你。

card.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来说明“说了什么、觉得什么、知道什么”：常见是动词后接that从句，如think/say/know that + 完整句子。that后面必须是有主语和谓语的完整内容。最常放在主句动词后，也可在口语中省略that。
card.usageNote: 语气通常中性，适合表达观点、转述别人说的话、说明自己知道的事，口语和一般写作都很常见。它不适合直接提问“是否”或“什么内容”时，那要换whether/if或wh-从句。that从句一般紧跟在动词后，不要被逗号隔开。初学者先稳用“I think that... / She said that... / We know that...”最安全。

slotSchema.label: 前面的动词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示想法、说法、知道的动词，如think, say, know, believe, hear。常错：用不接that从句的结构硬套。例：I think that he's right.

slotSchema.label: 连接词that
slotSchema.hint: 连接前面动词和后面的内容，本身不表达具体意思。常错：把that后面接成短语而不是完整句。例：She said that she was busy.

slotSchema.label: 内容从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整陈述句，要有主语+谓语。常错：写成that happy或that to go。例：We know that the store is closed.

commonErrors: I think that happy.｜错因：that后面不是完整句子，缺主语和谓语。｜改法：写成I think that she is happy.
She said that to leave early.｜错因：that后不能直接接to不定式来表达内容。｜改法：用完整句She said that she would leave early.
Do you know that where he lives?｜错因：that从句和wh-从句不能这样叠加。｜改法：Do you know where he lives?
I said, that I was tired.｜错因：动词和that从句之间通常不用逗号隔开。｜改法：I said that I was tired.
I think that is right he.｜错因：从句内部语序错误，英语从句仍用正常陈述语序。｜改法：I think that he is right.
I know whether he is here.｜错因：这里不是陈述已知内容，而是在说“是否”。｜改法：表示知道这一事实时用I know that he is here; 表示知道“是否”用I know whether he is here.

contrasts.title: vs whether / if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，that后接“陈述内容”，whether/if后接“是否”。场景上，that用于报告已知或主张；whether/if用于不确定、选择、间接问句。比较：I know that he is here. / I don't know whether he is here.

contrasts.title: vs wh- clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，that + 完整陈述；wh-从句用what/where/why/how等开头。场景上，that说“内容本身”，wh-从句说“哪一个/哪里/为什么”等信息缺口。比较：She said that she was late. / She said why she was late.

contrasts.title: vs noun + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，本卡是动词 + that从句；另一种是名词 + that从句，如the fact that...。语气上，动词结构更直接口语；名词结构更像说明概念，较书面。比较：I know that he left. / The fact that he left surprised me.

contrasts.title: vs zero that
contrasts.note: 结构上，口语里that常可省略：I think (that) ...；但正式写作或句子较长时保留更清楚。语气上，省略更口语；保留更稳妥。比较：I think he's right. / I think that he's right.

examples.sentence: I think that this book is useful.
examples.translation: 我觉得这本书很有用。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型。

examples.sentence: That she was tired, he already knew.
examples.translation: 她很累这件事，他早就知道了。
examples.note: 从句内容被提前，较少见，偏强调或书面；初学者平时优先用He already knew that she was tired.

examples.sentence: The report says that sales are rising.
examples.translation: 报告称销售额正在上升。
examples.note: 较常见于说明文和新闻式写作。

examples.sentence: I know that he is at home, but I don't know whether he is asleep.
examples.translation: 我知道他在家，但我不知道他是不是睡着了。
examples.note: 和whether形成对比：前者是已知事实，后者是“是否”。

examples.sentence: She said that she couldn't come today.
examples.translation: 她说她今天来不了。
examples.note: 日常转述别人说的话。

examples.sentence: We believe that the plan will work.
examples.translation: 我们相信这个计划会奏效。
examples.note: 适合讨论、会议、课堂发言。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
templates.template: I think that + 完整句子
slotHints: 后面放你认为是真的内容，要有主语和谓语，如 it is good / she is right
templates.exampleSentence: I think that he is right.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我觉得他是对的。

templates.label: 常用转述模板
templates.template: 主语 + said that + 完整句子
slotHints: 主语可用 she/he/my teacher 等；that后写对方说的内容，不用引号也可以
templates.exampleSentence: My teacher said that the test would be easy.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我的老师说考试会很容易。

templates.label: 另一常见位置模板
templates.template: 主语 + knows that + 完整句子
slotHints: 适合表达“知道某事实”；如果是“知道是否”，不要用that，改用whether/if
templates.exampleSentence: She knows that the shop is closed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她知道那家店关门了。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: The report says that + 完整句子
slotHints: 适合报告、文章、说明文字；that后放客观陈述
templates.exampleSentence: The article says that exercise improves sleep.
templates.exampleTranslation: 文章说运动能改善睡眠。

card.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来说明“哪一个人”或补充这个人的信息：先说人，再接who，再接一个缺少主语的从句。who在从句里指前面的人，常作主语。它通常紧跟在所修饰的人名词后面，放在句子中间或句尾，如the woman who works here。
card.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都很常见，尤其适合介绍人物、讲故事、说明某人身份。它最自然地修饰“人”，不修饰地点或物。正式写作中可更清楚地区分人物信息；很随意的口语里有时会省略改说两句短句。提醒：who从句必须紧贴它修饰的名词，不能被逗号外的别的成分隔开。初学者先安全使用“the person/man/woman/student who + 动词...”模板。

slotSchema.label: 被修饰的人名词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示人的名词，如the man, a teacher, students。不要放物或地点。常错：the book who...；应改为the book that/which...。例：the girl who sings well

slotSchema.label: who后面的从句
slotSchema.hint: 放一个缺少主语的从句，who在里面作主语，后面通常直接接动词。常错：the man who he lives here；去掉he。例：who lives next door

slotSchema.label: 主句其余部分
slotSchema.hint: 可在整个名词短语后继续完成主句。常错：把who从句放太远。例：The student who helped me is here.

commonErrors: the book who is on the table：who通常只修饰人，book不是人；改为the book that/which is on the table。
the man who he works here：who已经是从句主语，不能再加he；改为the man who works here。
I met who helped me yesterday：who定语从句前必须先有被修饰的人名词；改为I met the man who helped me yesterday。
the teacher is kind who teaches us：who从句不能随便后置，通常要紧跟名词；改为the teacher who teaches us is kind。
the girl who happy is：who后面需要完整谓语，不能只有形容词；改为the girl who is happy。
my friend, who lives next door is friendly：非限定性从句若前面有逗号，后面也要用逗号与主句隔开；改为My friend, who lives next door, is friendly。

contrasts.title: who vs that
contrasts.note: 结构上都可修饰人：the man who came / the man that came。语气上who更明确指人，也更自然；that更中性，口语常见。对比：the woman who called me较自然；the woman that called me也对。

contrasts.title: who vs which
contrasts.note: 结构区别是who修饰人，which修饰物或整件事。场景上说人物用who，说东西用which，混用会别扭。对比：the boy who won / the prize which mattered。

contrasts.title: who vs whose + noun
contrasts.note: who后直接接动词性从句；whose后要先接名词，表示“谁的”。场景上who说明这个人做了什么，whose说明这个人拥有什么。对比：the student who studies hard / the student whose book is missing。

contrasts.title: who vs where
contrasts.note: who修饰人，where修饰地点。结构上where后是关于地点里发生的事，不是人物动作。场景上介绍人用who，介绍地方用where。对比：the girl who works there / the cafe where she works。

contrasts.title: who从句 vs 两个短句
contrasts.note: 结构上who把两句合成一个名词短语；分成两句更口语、信息更松散。写作中who更紧凑，口语里两句也常用。对比：I know a man who can help. / I know a man. He can help.

examples.sentence: She is the teacher who helps us.
examples.translation: 她是帮助我们的那位老师。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型：人名词+who+动词

examples.sentence: The boy who sits by the window is my brother.
examples.translation: 坐在窗边的那个男孩是我哥哥。
examples.note: 定语从句放在主语名词后，整段再作主语

examples.sentence: I talked to a woman who works at the bank.
examples.translation: 我和一位在银行工作的女士聊过。
examples.note: 被修饰名词位于句子中间

examples.sentence: The scientist who discovered the method became famous.
examples.translation: 发现这种方法的那位科学家出名了。
examples.note: 较偏书面，适合写作

examples.sentence: The student who called me is here, not the one who emailed me.
examples.translation: 给我打电话的那个学生在这儿，不是给我发邮件的那个。
examples.note: 和另一个人作对比，体现识别功能

examples.sentence: Do you know the man who sells fruit on this street?
examples.translation: 你认识在这条街上卖水果的那个人吗？
examples.note: 日常问路/买东西场景很自然

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
templates.template: the + 人名词 + who + 动词 + 其他
slotHints: 如 person/man/woman/student
用动作说明这个人
templates.exampleSentence: the student who studies English
templates.exampleTranslation: 学英语的那个学生

templates.label: 句中位置模板
templates.template: I/We + 动词 + a/the + 人名词 + who + 动词 + 其他
slotHints: 主句先说遇见/认识/看到某人
who从句紧跟在人名词后
templates.exampleSentence: I know a girl who speaks Spanish.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认识一个会说西班牙语的女孩。

templates.label: 写作型模板
templates.template: The + 人名词 + who + 动词 + 其他 + 谓语
slotHints: 整个“人名词+who从句”作主语
后面再接is/was/became等谓语
templates.exampleSentence: The man who designed the bridge is famous.
templates.exampleTranslation: 设计这座桥的那个人很有名。

templates.label: 对比辨认模板
templates.template: the + 人名词 + who + 动词 + ..., not the one who + 动词 + ...
slotHints: 先锁定一个人
再排除另一个人
templates.exampleSentence: The girl who called you is here, not the one who texted you.
templates.exampleTranslation: 给你打电话的那个女孩在这儿，不是给你发短信的那个。
